_about: Curator hand-overrides applied on top of the automatic selection. track_pick forces an exact Discogs tracklist title (token-matched; enrich.py); video_pick forces a specific YouTube id (assemble.py, highest precedence); video_note adds a factual note; row_note appends a free-form markdown note; rating_override hand-sets the star rating after listening. Ranks are chronological (play) order.

video_note.6: listen is the sought-after **'94 Droppin' Science Remix** (Roni Size / DJ Die); the master links the canonical 1993 *Johnny*, which the '94 remix EP is catalogued under
video_note.7: the page's exact *We .R. 'E' (94 Mix)* upload (its title runs the letters together as 'We.R.'E'', which is why the auto-matcher first reached for the flip)

row_note.1: Discogs credits this **master** under the name-variant *Baby B Kane*; the pressing (White House WYHS 018) and the producer's usual spelling are **Bay B Kane** — same artist. Cut chosen from the 8-track EP for the set: the jungle roller *Jungle Warriors*.
row_note.7: A cornerstone of the sound: the *We Are E* riff (first cut 1991, before 'jungle' had the name) is one of the records the genre was built on; this is the 1994 Music House pressing of the '94 Mix.
row_note.14: Discogs credits this **master** under the spelling *Marvelous Caine*; the IQ Records pressing credits **Marvellous Cain** — same artist. Video is the original 1994 *Hitman* (the release also holds Dream Team, DJ Hype and Ascend & Dead Dred remixes).
row_note.28: Source pick was *Kool Skool* (its flip on Kickin' Underground KUS 16); the linked master is the sister pressing, whose shared cut **Horns Of Jericho** is the one carrying a page video.
